seoDescription: Detailed Carnotaurus walking on rocky volcanic terrain with ash clouds in the background. Free PDF.
author: Antigravity Agent
funFacts: Carnotaurus was a uniquely horn-faced theropod that lived in South America during the Late Cretaceous period.
Its name means 'meat-eating bull' due to the distinctive horns above its eyes. It was built for high-speed running.
It possessed extremely reduced, tiny forelimbs (arms) that were even smaller than those of the Tyrannosaurus Rex.
